# Welcome to the team

**EB-Lab** works with projects under [ClimChange-NewbornHealth](https://github.com/ClimChange-NewbornHealth). The public lab site is [estelablanco.cl](https://estelablanco.cl/).

## Mission

We build reproducible evidence on how **climate and environmental stressors** relate to early neonatal outcomes, alongside local and international partners. We combine epidemiology, public spatial/climate data when appropriate, and transparent statistical modeling.

## Coordination

| Role | Email | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lead science / maternal–perinatal health | [estela.blanco\@uc.cl](mailto:estela.blanco@uc.cl) | Scientific questions and priorities. |
| Reproducibility · repository management | [jdconejeros\@uc.cl](mailto:jdconejeros@uc.cl) | *Research collaborator – repository manager* (per org guidelines). |

::: {.callout-tip}
## ORCID
[Estela Blanco](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6232-9210) · [José Daniel Conejeros](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3402-4361).
:::

Useful links: [ClimChange-NewbornHealth](https://github.com/ClimChange-NewbornHealth) · [Lab website](https://estelablanco.cl/).

### Minimum expectations

1. Agree on shared data scope (time window, population filters, updates) **before** irreversible production code on large tables.
2. Prefer clear async updates when distributed: summary + findings + concrete questions.
3. Credit dependencies: if you reuse someone’s function in publishable work, cite them in code and outputs.

## Relation to official guidelines

Folder structure, `README`, license, and external data requirements are maintained in [Lineamientos_colaboradores](https://github.com/ClimChange-NewbornHealth/Lineamientos_colaboradores/tree/main/README.md). The manual summarizes but does not replace them.
